tool_use.param: ## Graphite-First Dispatch: Why Not a Separate Pipeline

## Graphite Status Caching in prepare_state()
<!-- Source: src/erk/cli/commands/pr/submit...[truncated 798 chars]...` means "checked and determined."
## Graphite-First Dispatch: Why Not a Separate Pipeline

session.tool_result: <tool_use_error>File has not been read yet. Read it first before writing to it.</tool_use_error>

1→--- 2→title: PR Submit Pipeline Architecture 3→read_when: 4→ - "modifying the PR submit workflow" 5→ - "adding new steps to the submit pipeline" 6→ - "debugging PR submission failures" 7→ - "understanding the Graphite-first vs core submit dispatch" 8→tripwires: 9→ - action: "adding a new step to the submit pipeline" 10→ warning: "Each step must return SubmitState | SubmitError. Use dataclasses.replace() for state updates. Add the step to _submit_pipeline() tuple." 11→ - action: "mutating SubmitState fields directly" 12→ warning: "SubmitState is frozen. Use dataclasses.replace(state, field=value) to create new state." 13→ - action: "adding discovery logic outside prepare_state()" 14→ warning: "All discovery (branch name, issue number, parent branch, etc.) must happen in prepare_state() to prevent duplication. Later steps assume these fields are populated." 15→ - action: "using ctx.invoke() with kwargs that don't match target function parameter names" 16→ warning: "Click ctx.invoke() forwards kwargs directly — any name mismatch causes runtime TypeError. Verify all parameter names exactly match the target function signature." 17→ - action: "calling an external tool that overwrites state without capturing it first" 18→ warning: "Save state BEFORE calling external tools that may overwrite it. Reference: capture_existing_pr_body() in submit_pipeline.py captures the PR body before gt submit overwrites it." 19→last_audited: "2026-02-16 14:20 PT" 20→audit_result: clean 21→--- 22→ 23→# PR Submit Pipeline Architecture 24→ 25→The PR submit pipeline is erk's reference implementation of the state threading pattern. This document explains **why** the architecture choices matter, not what the code does (read the source for that). 26→ 27→## Why Linear Pipelines: The Dual-Path Problem 28→ 29→Before the linear pipeline refactor, PR submission used branching orchestration with two paths (Graphite-first vs core). **The problem:** discovery code was duplicated between paths, issue linkage validation happened in multiple places, and error handling varied between branches. 30→
... [30 lines omitted] ...
31→Linear pipelines solve this by **deferring the dispatch decision**. Discovery happens once in step 1, then step 3 dispatches internally to Graphite or core flow. Both paths share the same state type and error handling. 43→3. **Type narrowing** — Later steps assume `branch_name` is `str`, not `str | None`, because step 1 checks and errors if unresolved

session.tool_result:
192→3. Set `markup=False` on Labels that show user content without links 193→ 194→### Static Widgets with Code/JSON Content 195→ 196→**Problem**: When displaying raw JSON, code, or file contents in a Static widget, `escape_markup()` may not fully prevent `MarkupError`. Content with Python type annotations like `list[str]` or complex nested escape sequences can still cause parsing failures. 197→ 198→**Error signature:** 199→ 200→``` 201→MarkupError: Expected markup value (found '\\"number\\"],\\n ...') 202→``` 203→ 204→**Solution**: Use `markup=False` on the Static widget instead of trying to escape content: 205→ 206→```python 207→# WRONG - escape_markup may not handle all edge cases 208→yield Static(escape_markup(json_content)) 209→ 210→# CORRECT - disable markup parsing entirely for raw content 211→yield Static(json_content, markup=False) 212→``` 213→ 214→**When to use which:** 215→ 216→- `escape_markup()` + `markup=True` (default): When you want Rich styling but need to escape user text (e.g., links with display text) 217→- `markup=False`: When displaying raw code, JSON, file contents, or any structured data that may contain brackets 218→ 219→## General Recommendations 220→ 221→1. **Use type annotations** - Textual uses Reactives heavily; proper types catch issues early
... [9 lines omitted] ...

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param:
- `markup=False`: When displaying raw code, JSON, file contents, or any structured data that may contain brackets

make: failed with exit code 2
## Summary
The `make fast-ci` command failed with exit code 2 due to a **Prettier formatting issue**.
### Failure Details
**Prettier Check failed:** - Code style issues found in **3 files** - The files that need formatting: - `docs/learned/integrations/github-review-decision.md` - `docs/learned/planning/incremental-dispatch.md` - `docs/learned/tui/status-indicators.md`
### Passing Components
All other checks passed successfully:
| Check | Result | Details | |-------|--------|---------| | AGENTS.md standard | PASSED | - | | CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md pairing | PASSED | 19 files checked, 0 violations | | Linting (ruff) | PASSED | All checks passed | | Format Check (ruff format) | PASSED | 1614 files already formatted | | Markdown Check | PASSED | - | | Exec Reference Check | PASSED | Reference is up to date | | Type Check (ty) | PASSED | All checks passed | | Unit Tests (erk) | PASSED | 5647 passed in 21.88s | | Tests (erk-dev) | PASSED | 136 passed in 4.05s |
... [7 lines omitted] ...

session.assistant:
Plan #8799 implementation complete. PR submitted: https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/8799
## Summary
Consolidated documentation from 11 erk-learn plans into 6 new docs, 2 updated docs, and auto-generated tripwires/indexes:
**New documents created:** 1. `docs/learned/cli/rich-table-output.md` — Rich table CLI output pattern with sparklines, linked issues, escaped text 2. `docs/learned/architecture/checked-out-branch-handling.md` — `is_branch_checked_out()` + `update_local_ref()` pattern for worktree-safe branch operations 3. `docs/learned/planning/incremental-dispatch.md` — Incremental dispatch workflow (local plan against existing PR) 4. `docs/learned/tui/filter-toggle-pattern.md` — 6-component pattern for TUI filter toggles (author filter as exemplar) 5. `docs/learned/cli/command-promotion.md` — 3-file pattern for promoting nested commands to top-level 6. `docs/learned/refactoring/coordinated-rename.md` — 12-point checklist for cross-codebase renames
**Existing documents updated:** - `docs/learned/cli/pr-submit-pipeline.md` — added Graphite status caching section - `docs/learned/textual/quirks.md` — added StatusBar `markup=False` fix entry
**Auto-generated updates:** 11 index and tripwires files regenerated via `erk docs sync` (830 total tripwires across 28 categories).
